source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.topic: Number bonds and related facts within 100
source_alignment.source_statement: Recall and use addition and subtraction facts to 20 fluently, and derive and use related facts up to 100.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack extends secure bonds within 20 into related tens and two-digit facts through structured part-whole, ten-frame and bar models.

objective.child_goal: I can use a known number bond to find related facts.
objective.statement: Recall bonds within 20 and derive related addition and subtraction facts within 100 using place-value structure.
misconceptions: Recounts every item instead of using a known bond.
Changes only one number when scaling a bond by ten.
Treats addition and subtraction facts from one part-whole model as unrelated.
Assumes the equals sign means the answer must appear last.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Two parts that combine to make a whole.
vocabulary.audio_script: A number bond links two parts to one whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two paths joining one labelled whole.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A fact we can work out from one we already know.
vocabulary.audio_script: A related fact keeps the same number relationship.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: One fact card branching into addition and subtraction cards.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Use what you know to work out something new.
vocabulary.audio_script: Derive means use a known fact to find a new fact.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A known bond lighting a new tens bond.

vocabulary.audio_script: Equal means both sides have the same value.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A balance level on both sides.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: You know 8 and 2 make 10. Say the missing part without recounting.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate fluent bonds within 10 and notice efficient recall.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Use the bond you know. Check with the frame only if you need it.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A ten-frame flashes, then leaves 8 and a hidden part.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build 7, 5 and 12. How many facts can this one model show?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Establish that one part-whole relationship generates related addition and subtraction facts.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The same two parts and whole can tell four connected facts.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A reversible part-whole mat with equation slots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: If 3 and 7 make 10, why do 30 and 70 make 100?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model deriving multiples-of-ten bonds by preserving the relationship while changing unit value.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Each one becomes one ten, so every number is ten times as large.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Single counters exchange for ten-rods in all three positions.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use a known complement to bridge through the next ten.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Six needs four to reach ten, so sixteen needs four to reach twenty.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check bond knowledge when the unknown is not at the end.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Work out the left side, then make the right side the same value.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A balance with quantity bars above each expression.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Confirm flexible movement between addition and subtraction using one model.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Show the parts and whole, then explain which part is hidden.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A bar model for 9, 6 and 15 with one section coverable.

manipulatives.purpose: Construct one conserved relationship and generate connected addition and subtraction equations.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Children place number cards into two parts and a whole, then tap, keyboard-step or switch-scan equation slots; invalid arrangements return cards without clearing correct work.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Large targets, spoken number/equation labels, colour-plus-pattern coding, no timer, undo, and an auto-place option make the reasoning executable without precise dragging.

manipulatives.purpose: Show how bonds scale from ones to tens and how equal expressions balance.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: A unit toggle exchanges counters for rods simultaneously; a separate balance mode compares two expressions with static quantity bars.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: The toggle has text and icon states, changes can be stepped one at a time, screen-reader announcements state every exchange, and reduced-motion mode uses before/after panels.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure recall within 20 first; then interleave fact families, multiples-of-ten scaling, bridging to the next ten and equations with unknowns in varied positions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retrieve and verify bonds within twenty without speed pressure.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: whole 6-20 + one known part + hidden part + optional frame check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Track strategy choice separately from response time; never penalise use of the model.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Generate two additions and two subtractions from one part-whole model.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: distinct parts + whole within 20 + reorderable equation cards
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Read symbols aloud and include equal statements in more than one orientation.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Derive bonds to one hundred from corresponding single-digit bonds.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: known a+b=10 + exchange all values to tens + missing multiple of ten
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Quantity must remain visually proportional and every value must scale together.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use complements to bridge tens and complete balanced equations with varied unknown positions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: teen plus complement or equivalent expressions + bar/balance support
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Exclude formal algebra notation; accept equivalent child explanations.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain bonds and transfer them across pictorial, symbolic and short context forms.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + prior bond + one changed representation + explain choice
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep contexts to one sentence and provide audio replay without speed scoring.

body.prompt: The parts are 7 and 5. The whole is 12. Choose the matching subtraction fact.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Start with the whole.
Cover the part 7; the other part is 5.
question_variants.explanation: Seven and five are the parts of twelve, so taking seven from twelve leaves five.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "12 - 7 = 5". Seven and five are the parts of twelve, so taking seven from twelve leaves five.
feedback.repair: Start with the whole. Cover the part 7; the other part is 5. Use that clue to repair facts seen as unrelated, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this part whole family, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "12 - 7 = 5": Seven and five are the parts of twelve, so taking seven from twelve leaves five.
feedback.misconception_check: facts seen as unrelated
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: 3 + 7 = 10. Use this to complete 30 + __ = 100.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Three ones became three tens.
Seven ones become seven tens.
question_variants.explanation: Scaling every number by ten changes 3 + 7 = 10 into 30 + 70 = 100.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 70. Scaling every number by ten changes 3 + 7 = 10 into 30 + 70 = 100.
feedback.repair: Three ones became three tens. Seven ones become seven tens. Use that clue to repair partial scale change, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this ten frame scale, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 70: Scaling every number by ten changes 3 + 7 = 10 into 30 + 70 = 100.
feedback.misconception_check: partial scale change
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Use 6 + 4 = 10 to find 16 + 4.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Sixteen has one ten and six ones.
The four completes the next ten.
question_variants.explanation: Six and four make ten, so sixteen and four make twenty.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 20. Six and four make ten, so sixteen and four make twenty.
feedback.repair: Sixteen has one ten and six ones. The four completes the next ten. Use that clue to repair recounts known bond, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this balance equation, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 20: Six and four make ten, so sixteen and four make twenty.
feedback.misconception_check: recounts known bond
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Complete 14 + 6 = 10 + __.
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Fourteen and six make twenty.
Ten plus what also makes twenty?
question_variants.explanation: Both sides must equal twenty, so the missing number is ten.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: 10. Both sides must equal twenty, so the missing number is ten.
feedback.repair: Fourteen and six make twenty. Ten plus what also makes twenty? Use that clue to repair equals answer last, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this balance equation, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: 10: Both sides must equal twenty, so the missing number is ten.
feedback.misconception_check: equals answer last
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Bond-lantern mission 1: complete 2 + □ = 6.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move named counters or record the child's indicated fact without supplying the missing number.
body.ten_frame_route: Use one or two structured frames with optional brief flash followed by an always-available static check.
body.part_whole_route: Use tactile or visual labelled PART, PART and WHOLE regions; correct cards remain in place during repair.
body.number_line_route: Use static labelled jumps to a target ten or between a known part and whole.
body.visual_route: One low-clutter model per panel with large numerals, generous spacing, pattern-plus-text labels and no colour-only meaning.
body.processing_route: Reveal one part, whole, scale or equation step at a time; preserve correct work and reduce choices when useful.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed placement can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.

gamification.mission: light a calm bond-map by building and checking one relationship
gamification.reward: one relationship lantern for a fact, model or check
gamification.retry_message: Your correct parts stay. Choose another model or clue and continue.

hints: Name the whole and the known part.
Use the structured frame or count from the known part only if recall needs checking.
question_variants.explanation: 2 and 4 are the two parts of 6. Check: 2 + 4 = 6.

feedback.correct: The two parts account for the whole. 2 and 4 are the two parts of 6. Check: 2 + 4 = 6.
feedback.repair: Keep the whole visible, cover the known part and reveal or count only the missing part; preserve all correct placements.
feedback.mathematical_evidence: 2 and 4 are the two parts of 6. Check: 2 + 4 = 6.
feedback.strategy_message: Recall, frames, part-whole models, number lines, pointing, eye gaze, AAC and adult-scribed explanations are equally valid; response speed is not evidence.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the recounts known bond idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Recounts every item instead of using a known bond.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child counts all counters for a repeatedly encountered bond.
repair_sequence: Name the known part and hidden complement.
Answer first, then reveal the frame to verify.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child retrieves two later bonds and uses the model only to check.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Changes only one number when scaling a bond by ten.
repair_sequence: Exchange each one for one ten in all three quantities.
Say each new value as the exchange happens.
Compare the complete original and scaled equations.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child scales a new bond with all values changed consistently.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats addition and subtraction facts from one part-whole model as unrelated.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child recalls an addition but guesses its corresponding subtraction.
repair_sequence: Build both parts inside the whole.
State whole minus covered part equals visible part.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child derives both subtraction facts from a new model.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Assumes the equals sign means the answer must appear last.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The child rejects or miscompletes an equation such as 10 + 5 = 8 + 7.
repair_sequence: Build both expressions as quantity bars.
Adjust the unknown until both totals match.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The child completes two balanced equations with unknowns in different positions.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain mat, muted patterns, optional sound effects and no celebratory bursts.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static before/after frames for exchanges, covers and balance changes.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Speak every number, symbol and equation with replay and describe the model in reading order.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep prompts to one short sentence; pair symbols with part-whole, frame or bar models.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show one relationship and one action at a time using a predictable build-hide-say-check routine.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited thinking time, stepwise reveal, undo and replay of each transformation.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Preserve correct placements during repair and describe errors as a relationship to rebuild.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach part, whole, bond, related, equal, tens and derive with gestures and matched models.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate bonds within 20 and related tens facts with models; secure requires retained derivation across fact families, bridging and balanced equations after spaced review.
evidence.parent_summary: Can use known number bonds to derive connected addition and subtraction facts, including facts with tens.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Retrieves bonds, explains one part-whole fact family, scales a bond by ten and completes equal expressions without relying on speed.
next_actions: Revisit unstable bonds with structured frames.
Apply secure bonds in two-digit addition and subtraction.
Continue equations with unknowns in varied positions.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 2 number-bonds pack with a deterministic 220-variant pilot bank. Four curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks develop fluent bonds within 20, complements, commutativity, inverse subtraction, missing parts, four-fact families, related multiples-of-ten facts and bonds to 100, bridging through ten, balanced equations, short contexts, misconception diagnosis, checking and spaced transfer. Every generated task offers ten-frame, part-whole and number-line routes, reduced-load SEND/dyscalculia supports, alternative inputs, rich corrective feedback and pressure-free missions without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected narrated contexts reference ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent mathematics, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
